comment: check:stubs phase-prose allowlist — the only phrasings in which a source comment may still name an implementation phase (spec §13.6). Every phase (§12) has completed, so a phase number can only ever be history: an exit-criterion citation, a §14 provenance note, or a frozen identifier. Anything else reads as deferred work and is a violation, because prose deferrals route around the STUB gate. Each pattern is a case-insensitive regular expression; matched substrings are removed from a line before the phase scan runs.

patterns.reason: Citation of the §12 phase plan itself
Exit-criterion citation — states what a test or surface was built to satisfy
§14 provenance note recording when a field or address form was introduced
Provenance about data persisted by an older build that must still hydrate
Phase 0 was the scaffold and its exemplars are named after it; never a deferral target
